key_insight: Any deterministic, fixed-radius, fixed-complexity local multiscale basis construction cannot achieve uniform convergence for all bounded-contrast coefficients; its worst-case Galerkin error stays O(1) as H→0.
engineering_relevance: This shows that scalable FEM-like multiscale surrogates need either expanding localization/spectral richness or richer coefficient access to guarantee robust accuracy on rough-media PDEs.
method: Defines a structural scalability model with bounded overlap and O(1) local coefficient visibility.
Proves a lower bound by comparing local patches with identical coefficients but different global correctors.
Uses periodic coefficient families, finite-dimensional approximation bounds, and mesh-density arguments to show the error floor does not vanish.
evidence_signal: The result is theoretical and worst-case: a fixed finite family of smooth periodic coefficients and smooth right-hand sides is used to prove a positive, H-independent lower bound on L2-to-energy Galerkin error.
watch_item: The impossibility result targets deterministic local constructions under fixed support and information radii, so practical methods may still work well outside this worst-case setting or by relaxing the locality constraints.
paper_title: An Order-One Lower Bound on the Error of Scalable Generalized Multiscale Finite Element Space Constructions
